text.title: Stakeholder Communication Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Session opens as Interview, drifts into Brainstorm, ends Socratic; stakeholder leaves confused.
antipattern.fix: Commit to one mode at session open; if a different mode is needed, schedule a separate session.

antipattern.symptom: All stakeholders treated identically; high-power-low-interest get the same cadence as low-power-high-interest.
antipattern.fix: Apply grid: high-power-high-interest = manage closely; high-power-low-interest = keep satisfied; low-power-high-interest = keep informed; low-low = monitor.

antipattern.symptom: Validation session ends 'yes, looks good'; next week the same stakeholder pushes back on what was 'agreed'.
antipattern.fix: Require email / Slack / doc edit before closing the session.

antipattern.symptom: PM uses 'why exactly do you want this?' during a final spec-confirmation session; spec re-opens.
antipattern.fix: Reserve Socratic for early discovery; Validation is convergence only.
